metric_assertions.assertion: container_cpu_usage_seconds_total rate increases by >= load_pct * 0.5 over baseline

trace_assertions.assertion: span.error_rate on egress to matched domain > baseline + 0.5 (DNS lookup failure)

observable_contract.name: dns_error
observable_contract.note: TODO: gRPC-only callers are filtered upstream; DNS resolver caches can mask the effect for >= TTL

observable_contract.contract: TODO: random-IP effect manifests as connection timeouts or wrong-host TLS errors; depends entirely on caller's retry/timeout config and TLS settings
observable_contract.name: dns_random

trace_assertions.assertion: span.error_rate on target endpoint > 0.9 during injection_window_s
span.http.method on target endpoint == new_method OR error_rate > baseline + 0.5 (method not allowed)
span.http.route on target endpoint changes to new_path OR error_rate > baseline + 0.5 (handler not found)
span.error_rate on target endpoint > 0.9 during injection_window_s

observable_contract.contract: TODO: body-patch effect on traces depends on client behaviour; manifestation typically client-side schema error, not directly observable in server spans
observable_contract.name: http_response_patch_body

trace_assertions.assertion: client_side parse_error_rate on target endpoint > baseline + 0.5

observable_contract.name: http_response_replace_body
observable_contract.note: TODO: contract is heuristic — parse-error visibility depends on client instrumentation; some clients accept empty body silently

trace_assertions.assertion: span.http.status_code on target endpoint == status_code for >= 90% of spans during injection_window_s

metric_assertions.assertion: container_cpu_usage_seconds_total rate increases by >= cpu_count * 0.5 cores

observable_contract.name: jvm_gc
observable_contract.note: TODO: requires JVM metrics exporter (Micrometer/JMX); without it, fall back to latency spike on co-located spans

observable_contract.name: jvm_memory_stress
observable_contract.note: TODO: stack-memory case typically surfaces as StackOverflowError; covered by span error_rate, not memory metric

trace_assertions.assertion: span representing method invocation has duration >= delay_ms * 0.9

observable_contract.name: jvm_method_latency
observable_contract.note: TODO: only methods that produce or are wrapped by an OTel/Jaeger span are observable; many internal helpers won't surface

observable_contract.contract: TODO: return-value override has no universal signature; downstream effect depends on whether callers validate the value (NPE, business error, silent corruption)
observable_contract.name: jvm_method_return

trace_assertions.assertion: db span on (db_name, table, sql_type) error_rate > baseline + 0.8
db span on (db_name, table, sql_type) duration >= delay_ms * 0.9

observable_contract.name: jvm_mysql_latency
observable_contract.note: TODO: only effective on Connector/J-instrumented apps; depends on db.statement attribute coverage in traces

observable_contract.contract: TODO: mutator effect is by definition app-semantic (changed constant, flipped operator, swapped string); no generic trace assertion holds. Use sn/ts/teastore-specific functional checks.
observable_contract.name: jvm_runtime_mutator

metric_assertions.assertion: container_memory_working_set_bytes increases by >= size_mib * 0.7 * MiB over baseline

trace_assertions.assertion: span.duration on cross-service call increases proportional to payload_bytes / rate_kbps

observable_contract.name: network_bandwidth
observable_contract.note: TODO: only payloads larger than buffer size will exhibit the cap; small RPC bodies pass through unaffected

trace_assertions.assertion: span.error_rate on edge(source_app -> target_service) > baseline + corrupt_pct/300 (TCP checksum-fail retransmits)

observable_contract.name: network_corrupt
observable_contract.note: TODO: TLS-wrapped flows treat corruption as connection reset; raw-TCP flows surface as application-level retry

trace_assertions.assertion: span.duration on cross-service call from source_app to target_service >= latency_ms * 0.5

observable_contract.contract: TODO: TCP duplicate-ACK handling absorbs most duplicates without symptoms; UDP-heavy traffic shows reorder/dup but spans rarely encode it
observable_contract.name: network_duplicate

trace_assertions.assertion: span.error_rate or retry_count on edge(source_app -> target_service) > baseline + min(loss_pct/200, 0.3)
span.error_rate on edge(source_app -> target_service) > 0.9 during injection_window_s

observable_contract.contract: TODO: time-skew has no universal trace signature; manifestation depends on app's timestamp use (JWT expiry, log skew, scheduled jobs). Best probe is process-level clock comparison via debug exec, not telemetry.
observable_contract.name: time_skew
